title: High Pressure Fuel Pump (HPFP) Diagnostic — GDI Engines
description: Diagnoses high-pressure fuel pump failures on Gasoline Direct Injection engines using live data comparison of rail pressure vs target across the RPM range. HPFP failures cause lean/no-start conditions that worsen with engine warm-up due to fuel vapor in the high-pressure circuit.
common_symptoms: engine stumble or surge at steady throttle
worsening performance as engine warms up
rough cold start with multiple HPFP pulses audible
metallic ticking noise from fuel pump area
physical_solution: Monitor HP fuel rail pressure via scan tool during cold start (where symptoms often absent) and after hot soak restart. Compare HP rail pressure to target/commanded value across RPM range. HPFP on GDI is cam-driven — test at various RPMs. LP fuel pump pressure (in-tank) must be confirmed good first before condemning HPFP. After replacement: adaptation reset recommended.

technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Partial — Mode $01 PID $0A gives low-resolution fuel pressure (3 kPa/bit, max 765 kPa). OEM-specific HP fuel pressure DIDs via $22 require ECM access on pre-gateway vehicles. P0087/P008A accessible via Mode $03 on all ELM adapters. Gateway blocks $22 on F/G-series BMW (ZGW), 2019+ VW (SFD). Most HP fuel pressure diagnosis requires OEM enhanced PIDs.
preconditions: LP fuel pressure confirmed adequate (50-80 psi)
engine hot soak condition reproduced if possible

procedure_steps.description: $19 02 FF — confirm DTC codes: P0087 (rail pressure low), P008A (LP circuit low), P008B (LP circuit high), P00C6 (LP pressure fuel injector control). Note: P008A suggests LPFP failure; P0087 with adequate LP pressure suggests HPFP failure.

$22 [LP_PRESSURE_DID] — read low-pressure fuel supply value. Target: 50-80 psi at all operating conditions. If LP < 40 psi at idle or drops under load: in-tank pump failing — replace LPFP before further HPFP diagnosis.

$22 [HP_PRESSURE_DID] — stream HP rail pressure at idle (target varies by OEM: 363-2175 psi at idle). At 2000 RPM target typically 1000-1500 psi. At WOT target 2000-3000 psi. HPFP is cam-driven — pressure increases with RPM.
$22 [HP_TARGET_DID] — read commanded HP target pressure alongside actual. If actual trails commanded by more than 20% at any RPM point: HPFP output is inadequate for demand.

Hot soak test — record HP pressure cold (should be high at idle). Drive until fully warm, turn off for 15-30 minutes. Restart and immediately record HP pressure. HPFP failure often worst on hot restart due to vapor lock in pump. If HP < 500 psi on restart with LP confirmed good: HPFP suspect.

Cam follower inspection (VW/Audi/BMW/Ford) — HPFP cam follower at top of HPFP housing. Remove pump, extract follower. Check follower face for wear (concave depression > 0.5mm = replace follower AND HPFP). Follower wear causes progressive HP pressure loss over time.

After HPFP replacement: $31 01 [FUEL_ADAPT_RESET_ROUTINE] — reset fuel pressure adaptation map in ECM. Clear all DTCs. Crank engine 3-4 times for 5 seconds each to prime HP circuit before extended crank.

Monitor HP pressure on new pump — should reach target pressure within 1-2 seconds of start. Verify P0087 cleared and does not return. Confirm hot restart now produces adequate HP pressure matching target DID.

oem_notes.Toyota/Lexus: HPFP on 2AR-FXE, 2GR-FXS, 8AR-FTS engines. DID 0x1201 at ECM 0x700 for HP pressure. Known ticking noise from HPFP on 2AR-FXE (2013+ RAV4 Hybrid) — noise alone is not cause for replacement if pressure meets target. ELM accessible pre-2019.
oem_notes.Honda/Acura: HPFP on 1.5T L15B7, 2.0T K20C4. Known P008A concern on 1.5T at high altitude — Honda service bulletin for LP pressure drop. HP pressure DID at ECM via HDS enhanced data. ELM limited for extended session access.
oem_notes.Nissan/Infiniti: HPFP on KR20DDET (Sentra Turbo), VR30DDTT (Infiniti Q50/Q60). LP circuit important — check LPFP filter at tank first. HP pressure DID via CONSULT. Infiniti VR30: cam follower inspection recommended at 30k mile intervals.
oem_notes.Ford: HPFP on all EcoBoost variants (1.5T/1.6T/2.0T/2.7T/3.5T). Cam follower wear pattern on 2010-2016 EcoBoost well-documented — Ford TSB issued. HP pressure DID via enhanced PCM. 2018+ SGW blocks ELM. Updated steel billet follower part number supersedes original.
oem_notes.GM: HPFP on 1.5T/2.0T Ecotec, LTG engines. Check in-tank LP pump first — P008A on 1.5T Equinox/Terrain is common failure. HP pressure DID via GDS2 enhanced. ELM limited for enhanced PID access.
oem_notes.BMW: N54/N55 HPFP known early failure pattern well-documented. B46/B48/B58 improved reliability. DID 0x2F21 HP pressure at ECM 0x12. Cam follower: check via dipstick hole on B58 or pump removal on N54. ZGW blocks on F/G-series — ISTA required for extended diagnostics. N54: HPFP replacement includes fuel line check valve inspection.
oem_notes.VW/Audi: EA888 2.0T TSI gen 1/gen 2: cam follower wear is well-known failure pattern. Follower inspection via pump removal or valve cover access hole (varies by model year). DID 0x0192 HP pressure at ECM 0x01. Pre-SFD ELM accessible. Updated follower and revised HPFP available from VW/Audi parts.
oem_notes.Stellantis: Hurricane 2.0T in Wrangler 4xe/Jeep Grand Cherokee. HP pressure DID via wiTECH enhanced data. 2018+ SGW blocks ELM access. Follower design improved on Hurricane vs earlier VW/GM cam-follower wear issues.
oem_notes.Hyundai/Kia: Theta II 2.0T/2.4T: HPFP issues less common than engine bearing concern, but P0087 appears at high mileage. Smartstream GDI engines have improved HPFP design. HP pressure DID [HKG_HPFP_DID] at ECM. 2019+ gateway blocks ELM. Kia Sportage/Tucson 1.6T: HPFP check during timing chain inspection interval service.
